allies.rationale_text: Cognitive Load Theory is a direct ally because bottlenecks often show up as working-memory limits that need chunking and scaffolding.
allies.source_quote: The central finding of CLT—that working memory is limited—is the primary cognitive bottleneck the theory seeks to manage.

allies.rationale_text: The Feynman Technique helps reveal the hidden intellectual bottleneck by forcing the idea into simple language.
allies.source_quote: It aligns with the bottleneck model by forcing the user to explain a concept simply ... immediately revealing gaps or blind spots—the intellectual bottlenecks—in their understanding.

antagonists.rationale_text: System-1's fast, intuitive, shortcut-driven processing directly opposes bottleneck thinking — bottleneck analysis requires the disciplined, slow, serial processing of System 2, while System 1's heuristics produce the pattern-jumping that misidentifies constraints and bypasses the real limit.
antagonists.source_quote: The disciplined, slow, and serial nature of processing at the bottleneck (System 2 thinking) stands in contrast to Type 1 thinking, which is fast, intuitive, and relies on mental shortcuts (heuristics).

structured_tensions.tension_text: Bottlenecks conflicts with optimization-theory when local utilization maximization destabilizes downstream queues.
structured_tensions.source_quote: bottlenecks conflicts with optimization-theory (optimization theory) when local utilization maximization creates downstream queue instability.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Bottlenecks conflicts with opportunity-cost when blocked capacity hides the throughput value being sacrificed elsewhere.
structured_tensions.source_quote: bottlenecks conflicts with opportunity-cost (opportunity cost) when blocked capacity hides foregone high-value throughput.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Bottlenecks conflicts with confirmation-bias when teams protect the chosen constraint diagnosis instead of testing alternate system limiters.
structured_tensions.source_quote: bottlenecks conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when teams seek evidence that confirms the chosen constraint diagnosis rather than testing the trade-off between current assumptions and alternative system limiters.

curation_notes.summary: Bottlenecks already had some compiled relation coverage, but reviewed curation strengthens provenance and keeps only the first-order surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor topology or multihop relation artifacts.
open_questions: Minto Pyramid Principle, Symphony, and law-of-the-instrument pressure remain source-real but were left deferred because the current canonical target surface is not yet stable enough.

allies: The raw ally section also names the Minto Pyramid Principle and Symphony, which may matter later if the canonical id surface expands.
antagonists: System-1 wired as antagonist (Task 4.1 sweep). Law-of-the-instrument → einstellung-effect already wired in Task 2.5.
